M-012.NAME: deploy-surface
M-012.path: deploy install.sh nginx docker-compose.yml scripts/phase36-resend-deploy-smoke.mjs scripts/phase38-de-mtproto-edge-smoke.mjs scripts/phase39-mtproto-availability-smoke.mjs scripts/phase39-mtproto-live-smoke.sh scripts/phase40-official-mtproxy-live-smoke.sh scripts/phase41-kpproton-faketls-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-36.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/plans/Phase-39.xml docs/plans/Phase-40.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml
M-012.owns: installation, deploy scripts, runtime composition, proxying, operator wildcard TLS install, Resend email provider env wiring, dev-only cert generation, host-tool preflight, AWG stealth profile generation, awg0 peer sync watchers, deploy-time secret handling, backup/restore, nginx hardening, DE-backed MTProto edge rollout, Phase-39 stale fallback guard, Phase-40 official MTProxy reference wiring, and Phase-41 KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime production wiring

disaster-recovery-role.role-1: Phase-22 uses the existing deploy/install surface as an operator-approved black box for replacement-server provisioning.
disaster-recovery-role.role-2: Phase-22 recovery planning must not redesign, edit, or bypass deploy/ or install.sh.

M-012.hotspot: H-015: Deploy idempotency must preserve WG keys, SSL certs, .env across repeated runs
H-017: Fresh deploys may generate obfuscation profiles, but upgrades must preserve profiles unless rotation is explicit

phase-36-plan.step-1: Ask for Resend API key and noreply@krotpn.xyz sender during clean production install.
phase-36-plan.step-2: Materialize EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and Resend env values into /opt/KrotPN/.env without printing the API key.
phase-36-plan.step-3: Fail deploy readiness explicitly when Resend is required but missing, preventing later provider_disabled registration errors.

phase-38-plan.step-1: Extend the deployment contract for a DE-hosted MTProto runtime while preserving the operator-approved install flow.
phase-38-plan.step-2: Add RU SNI router and DE runtime wiring only after static smoke proves HTTPS fallback and policy API safety.
phase-38-plan.step-3: Keep rollback to nginx-only public 443 documented before any public port ownership change.

phase-40-plan.step-1: Replace the DE KPprotoN runtime container with an official MTProxy data-plane launcher while preserving the operator-approved install flow.
phase-40-plan.step-2: Add only the narrow deploy/runtime wiring needed for official secret manifests, private stats, and RU HTTPS-vs-MTProto routing.
phase-40-plan.step-3: Keep all Phase-40 smoke output redacted and require public req_pq proof before release.

phase-41-plan.step-1: Disable official MTProxy as the active production data-plane and restore DE KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime wiring.
phase-41-plan.step-2: Restore RU public 443 SNI routing so krotpn.xyz web traffic reaches nginx and issued u-*.krotpn.xyz traffic reaches DE KPprotoN runtime.
phase-41-plan.step-3: Keep deploy/install flow operator-approved and limit changes to MTProto runtime selection, env rendering, and redacted smoke gates.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: Phase-35 changed the deploy surface so production installs consume validated operator wildcard TLS files instead of silently generating self-signed certificates.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: Clean VPS hotfix generates MTProto base secret and salt during deploy .env creation so backend Settings validation does not restart-loop after first compose startup.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-17: Phase-36 added redacted Resend deploy config handoff and /opt/KrotPN/.env generation for EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 plan adds DE-backed MTProto edge deployment as the next step after RU-only mtproto_proxy live blocking.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local deploy surface now renders HAProxy SNI router config, starts a DE runtime from deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, binds policy API to the relay address, and preserves MTProto base secret/salt/runtime token on redeploy.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Clean deploy follow-up keeps public admin 8443 separate from private web fallback 9443 and grants the HAProxy SNI router explicit permission to bind public 443.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: Live RU/DE deploy-surface follow-up applied the GitHub main fixes, regenerated runtime configs, rebuilt DE mtproto runtime, and verified public web/admin plus DE backend reachability.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-18: Phase-39 added a narrow deploy/live guard so stale DE_MTPROTO_DOMAIN_FRONTING=127.0.0.1:8443 converges to the private 127.0.0.1:9443 fallback without rotating MTProto secrets or redesigning install/deploy flow.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-19: Phase-40 planning records an official MTProxy runtime pivot after KPprotoN fake-TLS acceptance failed real client-level req_pq availability.
evidence.evidence-10: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design records the operator-approved rollback from official MTProxy to the KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI model used by the reference CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN project.
evidence.evidence-11: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local execution restored deploy/compose defaults to the KPprotoN DE runtime, selected deploy/haproxy-phase38.cfg as the active SNI router, and passed shell, compose, and static smoke verification.
evidence.evidence-12: 2026-05-19: Operator clean-deploy validation from GitHub main confirmed install/deploy surface works for wildcard TLS, Resend registration email, VPN, and KPprotoN MTProto proxy.
